# Pull Request

## Summary

<!-- What does this PR do? Link to the relevant issue if applicable. Closes #XX -->

## Type of change

<!-- Check all that apply -->

- [ ] `feat` — New feature (minor version bump)
- [ ] `fix` — Bug fix (patch version bump)
- [ ] `perf` — Performance improvement (patch version bump)
- [ ] `refactor` — Refactor without behaviour change
- [ ] `docs` — Documentation only
- [ ] `test` — Tests only
- [ ] `build` — Build system / dependency update
- [ ] `ci` — CI/CD change
- [ ] `chore` — Maintenance / tooling
- [ ] ⚠️ **Breaking change** — existing APIs change (major version bump)

## Checklist

- [ ] My PR title follows the [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/) format
- [ ] I have added / updated tests to cover my changes
- [ ] All existing tests pass (`npm test`)
- [ ] Lint passes (`npm run lint`)
- [ ] Format is consistent (`npm run format:check`)
- [ ] Build succeeds (`npm run build:all`)
- [ ] I have updated documentation where needed

## Breaking changes

<!-- If this is a breaking change, describe what breaks and how consumers should migrate. -->

N/A
